name: Arthur Willard Pryor
description: American bandleader, composer, virtuoso trombonist with the Sousa Band (1870–1942)
abstract: Arthur Willard Pryor (September 22, 1869 – June 18, 1942) was a trombone virtuoso, bandleader, and soloist with the Sousa Band. He was a prolific composer of band music, his best-known composition being "The Whistler and His Dog".

concerts.label: Fordham University Band: John Philip Sousa Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Amos White
about_quotes.evidence: I played a winter down there with the Verrett boys, Arthur and Albert Verrett. They had a seven piece orchestra down there... Arthur played the saxophone and clarinet. I played trumpet.

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I've got a (?) record here that somebody gave me up in Boston, of Arthur Pryor(singing)Aida.

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Mason
about_quotes.evidence: your greatest trombone player was Arthur Pryor. You see, they were the two guys.
